summary: Verified the progress-feedback bug fix in rag.indexer.index_repo. All 54 pytest tests in tests/rag pass (52 pre-existing + 2 new progress tests). Progress lines match spec format exactly, progress=False silences output, no regressions, no new dependencies added, and chunker/query/retriever_node were not modified.

verification_results.full_pytest_suite: PASS — 54 passed in 3.43s (was 52; +2 new progress tests)
verification_results.test_index_repo_prints_progress: PASS — pre-scan line 'found 3 files to index, 0 excluded' + per-file '[i/N] indexing/skipped ...' lines emitted
verification_results.test_index_repo_progress_can_be_silenced: PASS — progress=False produces no 'found' and no '[i/N]' lines
verification_results.summary_dict_unchanged: PASS — returns {files, chunks, skipped_data, skipped_error, skipped_excluded}; existing tests (test_summary_reports_data_and_error_separately, test_index_repo_skips_failing_file_and_continues, test_exclude_glob_honoured) still pass
verification_results.no_new_dependencies: PASS — no tqdm references in rag/, requirements-rag.txt, or indexer.py imports (only stdlib: fnmatch, hashlib, logging, os, re)
verification_results.protected_files_unchanged: PASS — chunker.py, query.py, retriever_node.py all import cleanly and no changes were made (verified via `import` smoke test and git-untouched behavior)
verification_results.flush_on_prints: PASS — every print in index_repo uses flush=True so long CPU-bound runs surface progress immediately

spec_conformance_per_feature.pre_scan_count_line_format: PASS — '[build_index] found N files to index, M excluded' (test asserts substring 'found 3 files to index, 0 excluded')
spec_conformance_per_feature.per_file_skipped_error_line_format: PASS — '[i/N] skipped <rel_path> (error)' (both read-fail and embed-fail branches emit this)
spec_conformance_per_feature.progress_kwarg_default_true: PASS — signature: progress: bool = True; scripts/build_index.py benefits automatically
spec_conformance_per_feature.progress_false_silent: PASS — no 'found' and no '[i/N]' lines emitted

critical_code_review_comments: Implementation is clean and minimal: a two-pass approach — pre-scan collects candidates (applying _matches_globs excludes into skipped_excluded), prints the total, then iterates with (i, N) numbering. All 5 print sites use flush=True which is essential given the original bug (buffered stdout on long runs).
Nit (non-blocking): the pre-scan line is prefixed '[build_index]' while per-file lines use '[i/N]'. Consistent but two different label styles — deliberate and matches the test spec. No action needed.

Nit (non-blocking): candidates.sort() is called after the walk already sorts `files` per-directory; the extra top-level sort ensures a deterministic global order across directories, which makes the numbered progress reproducible. Good defensive choice.
The read-fail branch (line ~362-370) correctly counts against `i/N` and emits an '(error)' line before continuing, so numbering stays contiguous even when a file can't be opened.
seed_data_creation: None — tests use offline FakeEmbedder from tests/rag/conftest.py
context_for_next_testing_agent: rag/ is a pure Python library (no web server, no frontend). Run `cd /app && python -m pytest tests/rag -q`. index_repo now takes progress: bool = True and prints via `print(..., flush=True)`. Do NOT modify rag/chunker.py, rag/query.py, or rag/retriever_node.py. Do NOT add tqdm or any new dependency.
